name: Punchbowl
desc: Working-class residential above downtown — National Memorial Cemetery, older homes, affordable for Honolulu.
insider: National Memorial Cemetery of the Pacific (Punchbowl Crater) is free and profoundly moving — over 53,000 veterans are interred here
Drive up Puowaina Drive for the panoramic view even if you don't visit the cemetery — it overlooks all of Honolulu
Housing here is some of the most affordable in metro Honolulu — modest homes within 10 minutes of downtown
The neighborhood is very quiet at night — almost suburban feel despite being so central to everything
Punchbowl Crater was a sacred ceremonial site in ancient Hawaii — 'Puowaina' translates to 'hill of offering' and it was used for important religious ceremonies
The lookout at the top of the cemetery is one of the best free viewpoints in Honolulu — most tourists skip it
Street parking is tight in the residential sections below the crater — most homes are older with single-car garages
Roosevelt High School is one of the most diverse public schools in the state — strong sense of community
The neighborhood lacks restaurants and shops — you're heading to Liliha or downtown for dining and errands
Punchbowl sits in a volcanic crater that's about 100,000 years old — the flat crater floor makes the cemetery eerily peaceful
